article_title: B.C. Reg. 42/2026 – British Columbia Energy Regulator
body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 42/2026
Made under: Energy Resource Activities Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 24, 2026

B.C. Reg. 42/2026, deposited March 24, 2026, under theENERGY RESOURCE ACTIVITIES ACT[sections 106, 107, 109, 111 and 112]. Regulation of the Board of the British Columbia Energy Regulator, dated March 20, 2026. The board of the British Columbia Energy Regulator orders that the Renewable Energy Projects Regulation, B.C. Reg. 28/2026, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — N. KIERNAN,Chair, Board of Directors. 1	Section 6 (1) (a) and (2) (e) of the Renewable Energy Projects Regulation, B.C. Reg. 28/2026, is amended by striking out "a project area" and substituting "the operating area or proposed operating area". 2	Part 4 is repealed and the following substituted: "class A project"means a renewable energy project with a rated nameplate capacity greater than 5 MW and less than 50 MW, other than a class C project; "class B project"means a renewable energy project with a rated nameplate capacity greater than or equal to 50 MW, other than a class C project; "class C project"means a renewable energy project that is a non-exempt reviewable project; "investigative use licence", in relation to a renewable energy project, means a licence of occupation under theLand Actthat is issued before an application for a permit or authorizations for the project; "major amendment"means an amendment to a permit for a renewable energy project to do one or more of the following: (a)	increase the rated nameplate capacity of the project; (b)	increase the total number of permitted wind turbines, solar arrays or battery or other energy storage systems; (c)	increase by one hectare or more an operating area of the permit; (d)	in the case of a wind energy project, increase (i)	the maximum permitted turbine height, or (ii)	the maximum permitted turbine blade size. 13.2The following fee must be submitted on application under section 24 of the Act for a permit for a renewable energy project: (a)	in the case of class A project, the sum of (ii)	the product of $500 and the rated nameplate capacity of the facility in megawatts; (b)	in the case of a class B project, $250 000;

minutes_briefs.title: BC updates renewable energy project classes and permit fees
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC Energy Regulator has amended the Renewable Energy Projects Regulation, changing how projects are classified and setting new permit application fees. Projects are now divided into class A (5–50 MW), class B (50 MW or more), and class C (non-exempt reviewable projects). The amendment also defines 'major amendment' and introduces a fee of $500 per MW for class A projects (up to $25,000) and a flat $250,000 fee for class B projects.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your renewable energy project's classification and budget for the new permit application fees.

key_dates.label: BC renewable energy project classification and fee amendment takes effect
